content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with atom indices labeled. Let me focus specifically on atom C:29 (carbon with index 29) which is located in the lower right portion of the molecule.

From the image, I can clearly see that atom C:29 is connected to:
- C:28 (carbon with index 28) - this is a bond in the ring structure
- C:24 (carbon with index 24) - another bond in the ring
- H:30 (hydrogen with index 30) - a hydrogen atom attached to it

Let me verify if there are any other connections by examining the bonding more carefully. Looking at the structure, C:29 appears to be part of a six-membered ring and has three bonds total: two to carbon atoms (C:28 and C:24) and one to a hydrogen atom (H:30).

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 2364, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_2364.xyz".

Based on my visual analysis, the neighbor atoms of atom C:29 are:
- Atom index 28, element C (carbon)
- Atom index 24, element C (carbon)  
- Atom index 30, element H (hydrogen)

The geomconf_index is 2364.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 29, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
